Title: Depicting the genetic and metabolic panorama of chemical diversity in the tea plant

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes glycosylation of flavonoid-related compounds; exact native substrate-product specificity not fully resolved in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Multiple flavonoid-related compounds in tea extract; caffeic acid glucoside/hydroxycinnamoyl-tyrosine/p-coumaroyl-cinnamoyl-glucoside/epicatechin/caffeoylshikimic acid/kaempferol derivatives affected in ex vivo assays
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Quercetin-3-D-galactoside; quercetin-3-glucuronide; methylsyringin; galloylglucose; other glycosylated flavonoid-related products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with natural variation of multiple flavonoids; functional alleles alter accumulation of glycosylated flavonoid-related metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contribute to tea quality traits and bioactive properties
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Modifies/flavor-diversifies target flavonoid products through glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Young leaves, buds, first leaf, third leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High expression in root, bud and first leaf
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; allele-phenotype association; transient overexpression in tobacco; ex vivo metabolomics enzyme assay with purified protein and tea extract
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A major QTL on chromosome 1 associated with multiple flavonoids contained CsUGTa. 2. CsUGTa belongs to the UGT708C subfamily and shows allelic variation linked to high- versus low-metabolite accumulation. 3. Transient expression in tobacco and ex vivo assays demonstrated that CsUGTa changes multiple flavonoid-related metabolites, supporting a role in flavonoid glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown tea plants; young leaves and third leaves sampled in July 2019
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; metabolite-based GWAS; RNA-seq-based SNP and expression data
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful allele target for metabolic engineering or marker-assisted selection to alter flavonoid glycoside composition in tea
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function supported by metabolite changes rather than a single fully defined native substrate-product pair; alleles CsUGTaH and CsUGTaL differ by five non-synonymous SNPs

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A major QTL on chromosome 1 associated with multiple flavonoids contained CsUGTb. 2. CsUGTb belongs to the UGT708C subfamily and shows allelic variation linked to high- versus low-metabolite accumulation. 3. Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes methylation of epicatechin gallate (ECG) to form epicatechin 3-(3''-O-methyl) gallate (ECG3''Me)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant for this methylated catechin product, but rate-limiting not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of ECG3''Me; functional allele A/C shows activity whereas G/G does not
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to tea chemical diversity and quality-related specialized metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High expression in root, second leaf and petal
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; authentic standard validation of metabolite identity; allele-phenotype association; cis-eQTL analysis; UTR InDel-expression-metabolite association; in vitro enzymatic assay with purified recombinant protein
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A major chromosome 7 QTL was associated with multiple metabolites including methylated catechin ECG3''Me. 2. CsCCoAOMT allelic variation and a 15-bp UTR InDel correlated with gene expression and ECG3''Me abundance. 3. In vitro assays showed CsCCoAOMT-A/C converts ECG to ECG3''Me, whereas the G/G allele could not.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown tea plants; leaf tissue context
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; mGWAS; RNA-seq expression data; eQTL analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding or engineering tea varieties with altered methylated catechin composition via favorable coding and UTR alleles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper states this molecular identity for ECG methylation was newly identified in tea plant
